# Polishing {.unnumbered} ![](images/wrangling.png){width="650" fig-align="center"}

**Cleaned data may require additional polishing.**

This might involve combining datasets from multiple sources, transforming, and imputing or removing missing values. ## Combining Dataframes can be combined along the vertical axis using ```concat()```. ![](images/concat.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} ::: {.columns} ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} df1 = pd.DataFrame({'A': ['purple', 'red', 'orange'], 'B': [1, 2, 3], 'C': ['C0', 'C1', 'C2'], 'D': ['dinosaur', 'drain', 'draw']}) df1 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
A B C D
0 purple 1 C0 dinosaur
1 red 2 C1 drain
2 orange 3 C2 draw
``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} df2 = pd.DataFrame({'A': ['green', 'blue', 'yellow'], 'B': [4, 5, 6], 'C': ['C0', 'C1', 'C2'], 'E': ['elephant', 'empty', 'easel']}) df2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
A B C E
0 green 4 C0 elephant
1 blue 5 C1 empty
2 yellow 6 C2 easel
``` ::: ::: ::: ::: ::: {.columns} ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Concatenate the two DataFrames result = pd.concat([df1, df2]) result ``` ::: ::: ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=5} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
A B C D E
0 purple 1 C0 dinosaur NaN
1 red 2 C1 drain NaN
2 orange 3 C2 draw NaN
0 green 4 C0 NaN elephant
1 blue 5 C1 NaN empty
2 yellow 6 C2 NaN easel
``` ::: ::: ::: ::: Dataframes can be combined along the horizontal axis using ```merge()```. ![](images/merge.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} ::: {.cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Merge the two DataFrames along rows merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C') merged ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ```{=html}
A_x B_x C D A_y B_y E
0 purple 1 C0 dinosaur green 4 elephant
1 red 2 C1 drain blue 5 empty
2 orange 3 C2 draw yellow 6 easel
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Merge the two DataFrames along rows merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C', suffixes=['_df1', '_df2']) merged ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ```{=html}
A_df1 B_df1 C D A_df2 B_df2 E
0 purple 1 C0 dinosaur green 4 elephant
1 red 2 C1 drain blue 5 empty
2 orange 3 C2 draw yellow 6 easel
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Merge the two DataFrames along rows merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C', suffixes=['_df1', '_df2']) merged ``` ::: :::{.callout-note} ### Learn more about the variety of join operations via the [documentation](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/user_guide/merging.html). ::: ## Transforming Datasets often need to be transformed between wide and long form data. ![](images/pivot.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} Pandas provides this functionality in the form of ```pd.pivot``` for long to wide: ::: {.cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.pivot_table( proteins, index='Subcellular Location', columns=['Function'], values='Molecular Weight (kDa)' ) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### The equivalent function for wide to long is ```pd.melt```. ::: ## Writing Just as we can read a variety of data types using pandas, we can similarly save pandas dataframes. ![](images/pandas-save.png){width="500" fig-align="center"} ::: {.cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} # loc_counts.to_csv('data/example_grouped.csv') ``` ::: ## In practice ::: {.callout-caution} ### Exercises 3.4 Combining datasets :::
